source: SuperLoop v2.0.0 migration review using retained v1.1.0 fresh-session output
limitations: This v2 migration review reuses retained v1.1.0 fresh-session output and was not a fresh v2 provider run; a full provider transcript, model attestation, and cryptographic process-isolation attestation were not retained.

judgments.expectation: Routes a finite composite task to the agentic path even though it is not recurring, while keeping one Loop and one Goal Contract.
judgments.evidence: The response explicitly selects an agentic finite Work Plan, assigns the complete outcome to one Loop and one Goal Contract, and says Parts are bounded work units rather than separate Skills.

judgments.expectation: Creates a fixed Work Plan whose Parts declare dependencies, exact acceptance criteria, and expected artifacts without creating a separate Skill or nested Loop per Part.
judgments.evidence: It supplies six stable non-placeholder Part IDs with an explicit dependency graph, durable artifacts, and substantive Part Gates, while explicitly prohibiting separate Skills and nested Loops.

judgments.expectation: Requires an independent Part Gate for each eligible Part and retries the same Part when that gate fails.
judgments.evidence: Each Part is handled by an isolated worker and a fresh independent evaluator, failed Parts are corrected against the same frozen definition, and each Part has a provisional two-retry cap.

judgments.expectation: Runs a distinct final Goal Gate against every exact Goal criterion only after all Parts pass, and does not equate Part completion with Goal completion.
judgments.evidence: The response enters a separate independent Goal Gate only after all Parts pass, checks every Goal Contract criterion, and states that passed Parts alone do not complete the Loop.

judgments.expectation: Treats a material scope or Work Plan change as a new proposal revision that requires review before execution resumes.
judgments.evidence: Material Part changes stop execution and require a parent-digest-linked revision, new digest-bound human decision, compile, validate, and supported migration; prior passes do not carry automatically.

judgments.expectation: Refuses to advance a locked or out-of-plan Part and does not silently reorder the approved Work Plan.
judgments.evidence: The response refuses to skip the failed Part, keeps later dependency-bound Parts locked, and forbids runtime reordering.

judgments.expectation: Retries the same eligible Part within its declared retry budget and binds evaluation to that exact Part definition.
judgments.evidence: It retains the exact Part-definition digest, corrects the same frozen definition within the remaining retry budget, and reevaluates it against the same criteria and commands.

judgments.expectation: Keeps every dependent Part locked until all declared dependencies pass their Part Gates.
judgments.evidence: The response states that dependent Parts remain locked and unlock only after a new passing result for the dependency is persisted.

judgments.expectation: Follows the declared blocked, inbox, or human-review policy when retry budget is exhausted instead of skipping the failure.
judgments.evidence: Blocked or human-required work is durably recorded and audited before reopening, while retry exhaustion stops for declared handling rather than permitting a skip or state edit.

judgments.expectation: Does not report the Loop completed before every Part passes and the final Goal Gate passes.
judgments.evidence: It explicitly says completion is impossible until every Part Gate and the independent final Goal Gate pass.

judgments.expectation: Classifies the open-ended request as continuous discovery and does not force it into a fake finite Work Plan.
judgments.evidence: The response selects continuous discovery, rejects a finite Work Plan and fake terminal date, and distinguishes finite daily runs from the continuing Loop.

judgments.expectation: Bounds sources, deduplication, items per run, time, retries, and cost so each run is finite even though the Loop continues.
judgments.evidence: It limits the approved-source allowlist to 20, blocks an empty allowlist, deduplicates by stable IDs and hashes, and caps candidates, accepted items, runtime, cost, and retries.

judgments.expectation: Requires durable state and independent evidence for accepted brief items.
judgments.evidence: It persists source watermarks, seen IDs, hashes, reasons, snapshots, verdicts, budgets, versions, and decisions, and requires independent item and whole-brief review.

judgments.expectation: Does not fabricate a terminal whole-Goal completion or final Goal Gate for an intentionally ongoing Loop.
judgments.evidence: The response never fabricates terminal completion, evaluates bounded daily output, and records no-work without calling the continuing task completed.

judgments.expectation: Keeps external publication or other high-impact effects behind explicit human authority.
judgments.evidence: It defaults to an internal draft and requires explicit human authorization for email, Slack, knowledge-base, or other external delivery as well as scope and budget changes.
